escalation.rationale: Active US-Israeli military operations against Iran continue with strikes on energy infrastructure and naval targets, Iran blocking Strait of Hormuz, and oil prices reaching $112/barrel.

ticker_items: Iran claims F-35 shot down, releases military video
IRGC spokesman Ali Mohammad Naini killed in US-Israeli attack
Oil hits $112/barrel as Iran targets Gulf refineries
Pentagon seeks $200 billion for Iran war funding
European allies refuse military support for Iran operations

eastern_europe.description: Russian drones struck apartment buildings, causing fires and injuring at least three people.

middle_east.description: Double Iranian missile strike on world's largest LNG facility causing extensive damage.

middle_east.target_location: Mina Abdullah and Mina Al-Ahmadi refineries
middle_east.description: Drone attacks on two major Kuwaiti oil refineries sparked fires.
Israeli attack on Iranian gas facility, according to US officials who claim no prior knowledge.
Israeli air strikes hit Iranian naval vessels on Caspian Sea coast
Iran hits multiple Gulf oil refineries in retaliation

claimed_totals.figure: 1,444 dead in Iran, 18 in Israel, 13 US soldiers, 21 in Gulf states

claimed_totals.claim: Total deaths from US-Israel attacks on Iran

ukraine.text: Iran and Russia are democratising suicide attack drones to such an extent that soon aerial strikes will be within the reach of terrorists
ukraine.speaker: Volodymyr Zelensky
ukraine.title: President of Ukraine

middle_east.text: NATO allies benefit far more from reopening of Strait of Hormuz than US
middle_east.speaker: White House Press Secretary
middle_east.title: Press Secretary

middle_east.text: Iran was not rebuilding enrichment prior to war
middle_east.speaker: Tulsi Gabbard
middle_east.title: US Intelligence Chief

middle_east.text: Iran has not gotten anywhere close to developing nuclear weapons over the last year
middle_east.speaker: Joe Kent
middle_east.title: Former Counterterrorism Director

observers.description: EU leaders holding emergency summit in Brussels to address Iran war fallout and rising energy prices. European nations demanding reopening of Strait of Hormuz and moratorium on energy infrastructure strikes. Hungary blocking loan package to Ukraine amid regional crisis.

Britain saying no to Trump's Iran war despite historic US-UK relationship. UK refusing to commit military aid to Iran conflict operations. Nigerian President conducting historic visit to UK amid regional tensions.
Asian countries including those near China are scrambling to conserve energy as Iran war disrupts oil and gas supplies, raising concerns about regional stability and economic impacts.

financial.notes: Oil prices jumped to $112/barrel as Iran conflict widens and energy infrastructure comes under fire, with Gulf states losing an estimated $15.1 billion in energy revenues.

update_log.text: Iran releases video allegedly showing air defense systems hitting US F-35 aircraft
IRGC spokesman Ali Mohammad Naini reported killed in US-Israeli attack
Israel widens attacks to northern Iran, hitting naval vessels on Caspian Sea coast
Pentagon reportedly seeking $200 billion from Congress for Iran war funding
Iran launches double strike on Qatar's largest LNG facility causing extensive damage
Kuwaiti oil refineries hit by Iranian drone attacks, emergency crews controlling fires
Trump threatens destruction of Iran's South Pars gas field if attacks continue
EU leaders demand Strait of Hormuz reopening at Brussels emergency summit
